mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the vLLM CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface, which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with gcloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys vLLM on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.18.0 --namespace vllm --create-namespace

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> Uninstalls the vLLM release.
> Deletes the vLLM namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp vllm-pod:/path/to/data /local/backup

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.18.0 --namespace vllm

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Backup your data from the vLLM pod.
> Upgrade vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the new pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or reduce the resource requests for the vLLM deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials' to refresh your kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved PagedAttention performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed, along with sufficient resources for the cluster.
